dependent_vars.definition: per-item correctness on the kernel-covered definitional-QA slice (pinned item order; non-LLM rubric)
dependent_vars.name: item_correct

dependent_vars.definition: per-item correctness on the externally-authored M0b-filtered slice (RT-7a Holm-corrected secondary)
dependent_vars.name: item_correct_external

dependent_vars.definition: task + kernel-covered-slice accuracy (full metric vector V, F0 accounting)
dependent_vars.name: accuracy

dependent_vars.definition: total-system peak ledger per F0 §3.4 (V component)
dependent_vars.name: memory

dependent_vars.definition: FLOPs/query, p50/p95 latency, usd/query per F0 §3.3 — verifier NN-cleanup |lexicon|·D MACs on the ledger (V component)
dependent_vars.name: inference_compute

n_planned.extension_predicate: R3 extension fires iff primary_reject@R1 AND primary_reject@R2 (machine predicate over frozen fields); an extension rung may only STRENGTHEN an already-satisfied conjunction, never substitute into it
n_planned.iface_gate: P10: >=300 labelled outputs per rung; extraction-failure Wilson-LB > 10% ⇒ INSTRUMENT-INVALID (instrument events, never hypothesis events; no free wins or losses)
n_planned.power: n=500 items: power ≈0.998 at h=0.2; TOST branch powered ≥0.90 at the h=0.2 margin (P8 §1.6: n≈271 suffices)
n_planned.rung_set_claim: HE1 conjunctive rung-pair set declared at freeze: [(R1,R2),(R2,R3)] — tested under IUT with exactly this membership (P1 §0 rung-set discipline, RT-12)

endpoints.test: paired item bootstrap (BCa, B=10000, PRNG seed 20260708), one-sided α=0.05: the one-sided 95% lower bound of gap_closed(R1,R2) must clear the pre-registered 0.5 bar; best retry budget k∈{1,2,4} reselected inside every resample (selection inside the CI, P8 §1.7); estimand per P8 §1.2
one-sided paired bootstrap vs arm 10 at matched retry budget and FLOPs; Holm within F-secondary(f2) at α=0.05 (RT-2: HE1 cannot PASS without this)
one-sided paired bootstrap vs the kernel-as-text arm; Holm within F-secondary(f2)
gap_closed(R2,R3) vs the 0.5 bar, same machinery; second member of the frozen HE1 rung-pair set (IUT)

HE2: verifier-gated cascade must beat BOTH the logprob gate AND the gloss-text self-check gate at EVERY pre-registered escalation budget (IUT across budgets: family p = worst budget p; per P1 HE2 kill text)
HC3: kernel verifier vs trained-PRM arm at matched FLOPs, one-sided paired bootstrap, minimum effect h=0.2; PRM-parity as a positive claim requires TOST at the same margin
RT-7a ecological-validity secondary on the externally-authored slice; Holm within F-secondary(f2)
C-4 robustness gate: >=4/5 sampling seeds same-direction verifier lift (pre-declared secondary, not a test)
P10 extraction-failure instrument gate, success-direction rendering: extraction-success Wilson-LB must clear 0.90 at n>=300 labelled outputs per rung (equivalently failure Wilson-LB <= 0.10)

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HE1 (verbatim): Measured range: (135M,360M),(360M,1.7B). Envelope: 3 rungs ⇒ gap-closure-fraction trend to 7B, direction-only; bias stated: the s→S gap itself narrows as S grows. Anchor: Cascade/verification-routing literature measured to GPT-4-class (FrugalGPT-style) licenses mechanism existence, not effect size, above 7B. Row HE2 (verbatim): Measured range: 135M→1.7B cascade. Envelope: Same as HE1; the cascade topology is standard at frontier scale, only the kernel gate's marginal value needs re-measurement. Anchor: Same anchors as HE1. Row HC3 (verbatim): Measured range: 135M–1.7B. Envelope: Relative kernel-vs-PRM verdict extrapolates to ~7B ONLY under the stated assumption that the PRM is held at its measured size; a frontier-scale PRM re-opens the fork. Anchor: PRM literature (process supervision) improves with PRM scale; verdict is indexed to the PRM size class tested.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §3 HE1 kill criterion (track text + RT-2 amendment, verbatim): 'dead at a rung if (a) <50% of the s→S gap closed at best pre-registered retry budget, OR (b) the text null, matched-compute sampling, or the gloss-text self-verify + retry arm closes as much gap at ≤ the same FLOPs/query — HE1 cannot PASS without the kernel-verifier arm beating arm 10 at matched budget, OR (c) closing the gap costs ≥ running model_S directly. Nulls require TOST.' HE2 rider (verbatim): 'Dead if not strictly dominant over BOTH the logprob gate AND the gloss-text self-check gate — dominance means the verifier-gated frontier point is above each competing gate's at every pre-registered escalation budget (per-budget paired bootstrap, Holm-corrected across budgets, α=0.05); any budget where either competing gate wins breaks dominance.' HC3 rider (verbatim): 'If the generic PRM matches the kernel verifier on kernel-covered content at matched FLOPs, the kernel's verification contribution collapses into commodity verification; determinism/provenance revert to governance claims and HC3 (and M1/M5's kernel-specificity) is dead.' HS12 has no kill — latency (batch-1 and throughput modes) selects the surviving implementation.

corpus_hashes._recipe: sha256 over the sorted concatenation of '<relpath>:<file-sha256>\n' lines for every file under data/<corpus>/
corpus_hashes.external-eval-slice: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-ext (M0b-filtered public-benchmark subset; WiC -> OpenBookQA -> MMLU preference order, GNG-0 ratification default)
corpus_hashes.extraction-labelled-set: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-xif (>=300 labelled outputs per rung, P10)
corpus_hashes.gloss-corpus-and-rag-index: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:d-qa (ops amendment before any final-phase run)

pins.harness_manifest: PINNED-AT-INPUTS:f2.inputs (modal staged-bytes manifest sha, modal_common convention)

runner_constraints.hardware: modal cloud GPU; image + GPU class pinned in the f2.inputs staged-bytes harness manifest (I-MODAL rebuild from requirements-image.txt)

title: F2 — the pivot: verifier-offload buys parameters (HE1 primary; HE2 cascade, HC3 PRM, HS12 placement riders); SmolLM2 135M/360M/1.7B; P10 extraction-instrument gate; gloss-text self-verify+retry arm per P7 RT-2
